ksa_text: Knowledge of AI security threat modeling and attack vectors, including prompt injection, data exfiltration, model poisoning, supply chain vulnerabilities, and threat assessment methodologies specific to machine learning systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA SP-K-001 (Security and Privacy/Knowledge) excluded as out_of_scope (composite 0.455, highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.455). Domain prior: out. Core hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Exclude consistent with out_of_scope classification, composite 0.455, and Security and Privacy (SP) handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence challenge the exclusion of this Security and Privacy KSA (composite 0.455)?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Market analysis of ISO 8000 Data Manager job postings does not surface this Security and Privacy competency as a standard requirement at the relevant level. Exclusion consistent with role focus.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Exclusion upheld. Market evidence consistent with exclusion. Final decision: exclude.
